description: BOLA (also known as IDOR) occurs when a resource ID accepted via the URL is used to fetch or modify a record without checking that the authenticated user actually owns or has rights to that record.
fix_description: After fetching the requested object from the database, compare its owner_id (or equivalent) with the authenticated user's id. If they do not match and the caller is not an admin, return HTTP 403.
patch_steps: Add authentication (see broken_auth template) so that current_user is available in the handler.
Fetch the object from the database using the supplied id.
If the object does not exist, return HTTP 404.
Compare object.owner_id with current_user.id; if they differ and the user is not an admin, return HTTP 403.
Only return the object after the ownership check passes.
verification_rules: Every handler that accepts an object id path parameter performs an ownership check
Accessing another user's resource returns HTTP 403, not 200
Admin role bypass is explicit and audited
Tests cover both the authorised and unauthorised access scenarios
references: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
